observable_failures: When a `.netrc` file exists for a target host, the `uri` module overrides a user-specified `Authorization` header with Basic auth credentials from `.netrc`, resulting in a 401 Unauthorized response.
Despite manually setting an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header in the `uri` module, the request defaults to `.netrc`-based Basic auth.
repair_targets: Add a `use_netrc` parameter (defaulting to `true`) to the `uri` module and underlying request logic so that when set to `false`, `.netrc` credentials are ignored and any explicitly set `Authorization` headers are respected.
regression_expectations: When `use_netrc` is not specified or is set to `true`, existing `.netrc`-based authentication behavior must continue to work as before.

requirements.text: `Request.__init__` must expose a parameter that determines if `.netrc` credentials are considered and store that preference for later use in request handling.
requirements.findings: The `Request.__init__` method (lib/ansible/module_utils/urls.py:1307-1310) accepts 16 parameters but none of them is a `use_netrc` (or equivalent) parameter. No netrc-related instance attribute is stored anywhere in `__init__` (lines 1329-1351). The only netrc-related code in the entire file is in `Request.open`, at lines 1488-1497, where `.netrc` is unconditionally consulted as a fallback when no explicit username/password is provided — there is no flag to suppress this behavior. The requirement to expose a `use_netrc` parameter in `__init__` and store it as an instance attribute for later use is entirely absent.

requirements.text: `Request.open` should accept a control for `.netrc` usage, apply fallback when not provided, and only attempt to read `.netrc` if allowed.
requirements.findings:
The `Request.open` method signature (lines 1358–1364) accepts 23 parameters but none of them is a `use_netrc` (or equivalent) parameter. The fallback block (lines 1416–1432) applies `self._fallback(value, self.attr)` for every other per-call override — `use_proxy`, `validate_certs`, `force_basic_auth`, etc. — but there is no corresponding `use_netrc` local variable resolved from a call-site argument or an instance attribute.

The `.netrc` read sits inside the bare `else` branch (line 1487) that is reached whenever no `username` was resolved from the URL or from `url_username`/`url_password`. It is unconditional: `netrc.netrc(os.environ.get('NETRC'))` is always called (wrapped only in an `IOError` guard at line 1491), and if credentials are found they are injected into the `Authorization` header at line 1497 with no way for the caller to suppress this. There is no `if use_netrc:` guard, no instance attribute consulted, and no parameter in the signature that could carry such a preference. All three parts of the requirement are absent:
1. No `use_netrc` parameter in the `open` signature (lines 1358–1364).
2. No fallback resolution (`self._fallback(use_netrc, self.use_netrc)`) that would apply the instance default when the call-site omits the argument (lines 1416–1432).
3. No conditional guard around the `netrc.netrc()` call that would skip it when the flag is `False` (lines 1487–1497).

requirements.text: `open_url` has to define a parameter for `.netrc` handling, default it to enabled, and forward the value when creating a `Request`.
requirements.findings:
The `open_url` function (lines 1649–1668) is entirely missing all three elements required by req-003:

1. **No `use_netrc` parameter in the signature** — the function signature (lines 1649–1655) lists 23 parameters (`url`, `data`, `headers`, `method`, `use_proxy`, `force`, `last_mod_time`, `timeout`, `validate_certs`, `url_username`, `url_password`, `http_agent`, `force_basic_auth`, `follow_redirects`, `client_cert`, `client_key`, `cookies`, `use_gssapi`, `unix_socket`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`) but `use_netrc` is not among them.

2. **No default value** — because the parameter does not exist, there is no `use_netrc=True` default.

3. **No forwarding to `Request.open`** — `Request()` is instantiated with no constructor arguments at line 1662, and the subsequent `.open(...)` call (lines 1662–1668) passes all 23 other keyword arguments explicitly but omits `use_netrc` entirely. There is no path by which a caller could suppress `.netrc` usage through `open_url`.

requirements.text: `fetch_url` needs to accept a parameter that signals whether `.netrc` is used, default it to enabled, and pass it along to `open_url`.
requirements.findings:
The `fetch_url` function (lib/ansible/module_utils/urls.py:1818–1821) accepts 16 parameters but `use_netrc` is entirely absent from the signature. There is no netrc-related parameter with any name or default value.

The single `open_url` call inside `fetch_url` (lines 1898–1905) forwards 23 keyword arguments explicitly — `data`, `headers`, `method`, `use_proxy`, `force`, `last_mod_time`, `timeout`, `validate_certs`, `url_username`, `url_password`, `http_agent`, `force_basic_auth`, `follow_redirects`, `client_cert`, `client_key`, `cookies`, `use_gssapi`, `unix_socket`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers` — but `use_netrc` is not among them.

All three elements required by req-004 are absent:
1. No `use_netrc` parameter in the `fetch_url` signature (lines 1818–1821).
2. No `use_netrc=True` default value.
3. No forwarding of `use_netrc` to `open_url` (lines 1898–1905).

requirements.text: `url_get` should define a parameter for `.netrc` usage and explicitly forward this preference to `fetch_url` in all calls.
requirements.findings:
The `url_get` function signature at get_url.py:382-383 was read and confirmed. It accepts exactly 13 parameters: `module`, `url`, `dest`, `use_proxy`, `last_mod_time`, `force`, `timeout`, `headers`, `tmp_dest`, `method`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. No `use_netrc` parameter is present.

The single `fetch_url` call at lines 391-392 was read and confirmed. It forwards: `use_proxy`, `force`, `last_mod_time`, `timeout`, `headers`, `method`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. No `use_netrc` keyword argument is forwarded.

The `fetch_url` signature at urls.py:1818-1821 was read and confirmed. Its parameters are: `module`, `url`, `data`, `headers`, `method`, `use_proxy`, `force`, `last_mod_time`, `timeout`, `use_gssapi`, `unix_socket`, `ca_path`, `cookies`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. No `use_netrc` parameter exists there either.

The netrc block at urls.py:1488-1497 was read and confirmed. It is unconditional — it runs whenever neither `url_username` nor `force_basic_auth` is set, with no gate to skip it.

All three elements required by req-005 are absent:
1. No `use_netrc` parameter in the `url_get` signature (lines 382-383).
2. No `use_netrc=True` default value.
3. No forwarding of `use_netrc` to `fetch_url` in the call at lines 391-392.

requirements.text: The main flow of `get_url` must retrieve the `.netrc` setting from module arguments and consistently forward it to `url_get` for both primary download and checksum retrieval.
requirements.findings:
All three elements required by req-006 are entirely absent from `get_url.py`.

1. **No `use_netrc` in the argument spec** — `main()` builds its argument spec via `url_argument_spec()` and then `argument_spec.update(...)` (lines 468–479). The update block adds `url`, `dest`, `backup`, `checksum`, `timeout`, `headers`, `tmp_dest`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, and `ciphers`, but no `use_netrc` key. A full-file grep for `use_netrc` returns zero matches.

2. **No retrieval from `module.params`** — The param-extraction block (lines 488–499) reads `url`, `dest`, `backup`, `force`, `checksum`, `use_proxy`, `timeout`, `headers`, `tmp_dest`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, and `ciphers`. There is no `use_netrc = module.params['use_netrc']` line anywhere in `main()`.

3. **Not forwarded to either `url_get` call** — There are exactly two calls to `url_get` in `main()`:
   - Checksum retrieval at lines 523–524: forwards `use_proxy`, `last_mod_time`, `force`, `timeout`, `headers`, `tmp_dest`, `unredirected_headers`, `ciphers` — no `use_netrc`.
   - Primary download at lines 601–602: forwards `use_proxy`, `last_mod_time`, `force`, `timeout`, `headers`, `tmp_dest`, `method`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress` — no `use_netrc`.

Because `use_netrc` is absent from the argument spec, from param extraction, and from both `url_get` call sites, the requirement is entirely unimplemented.

requirements.text: The `uri` function requires a parameter controlling `.netrc` behavior and should propagate it to the lower-level request call.
requirements.findings:
The `uri` function at lib/ansible/modules/uri.py:547-548 accepts exactly 12 parameters: `module`, `url`, `dest`, `body`, `body_format`, `method`, `headers`, `socket_timeout`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. No `use_netrc` parameter (or any equivalent) is present.

The single `fetch_url` call inside `uri` (lines 569-573) forwards: `data`, `headers`, `method`, `timeout`, `unix_socket`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `use_proxy`, `decompress`, `ciphers`, and an optional `last_mod_time` via `**kwargs`. `use_netrc` is not among the forwarded arguments.

A full-text search for `use_netrc` in uri.py returns zero matches — the string does not appear anywhere in the file.

All three elements required by req-007 are absent:
1. No `use_netrc` parameter in the `uri` function signature (lines 547-548).
2. No `use_netrc=True` default value.
3. No forwarding of `use_netrc` to `fetch_url` in the call at lines 569-573.

requirements.text: The entry point of `uri` has to include `.netrc` in its accepted arguments and forward the captured value into the `uri` function.
requirements.findings:
The `main()` function in `lib/ansible/modules/uri.py` is the entry point for the `uri` module. All three elements required by req-008 are entirely absent.

1. **No `use_netrc` in `argument_spec`** — `argument_spec.update()` at lines 587–608 adds 20 keys (`dest`, `url_username`, `url_password`, `body`, `body_format`, `src`, `method`, `return_content`, `follow_redirects`, `creates`, `removes`, `status_code`, `timeout`, `headers`, `unix_socket`, `remote_src`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`). `use_netrc` is not among them. A full-text grep for `use_netrc` in `uri.py` returns zero matches — the string does not appear anywhere in the file.

2. **No retrieval from `module.params`** — The param-extraction block (lines 616–630) reads `url`, `body`, `body_format`, `method`, `dest`, `return_content`, `creates`, `removes`, `status_code`, `timeout`, `ca_path`, `headers`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. There is no `use_netrc = module.params['use_netrc']` line.

3. **Not forwarded to `uri()`** — The call to `uri()` at lines 672–674 passes exactly 12 positional arguments: `module`, `url`, `dest`, `body`, `body_format`, `method`, `dict_headers`, `socket_timeout`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `decompress`, `ciphers`. `use_netrc` is not among them, and the `uri()` function itself also lacks the parameter (confirmed by req-007 findings).

requirements.text: The `run` method of the `url` lookup plugin must respect the configured `.netrc` option and forward it to the underlying request logic.
requirements.findings:
The `run` method of the `url` lookup plugin (`lib/ansible/plugins/lookup/url.py:209-248`) has no `use_netrc` option anywhere in the plugin. Three distinct layers are all absent:

1. **DOCUMENTATION / option spec (lines 7-167)**: Defines 17 options (`validate_certs`, `split_lines`, `use_proxy`, `username`, `password`, `headers`, `force`, `timeout`, `http_agent`, `force_basic_auth`, `follow_redirects`, `use_gssapi`, `unix_socket`, `ca_path`, `unredirected_headers`, `ciphers`, `_terms`). `use_netrc` is not among them, so no caller can configure it.

2. **`run` method body (lines 209-248)**: Calls `open_url` with 15 explicit keyword arguments (lines 218-232). Every option from DOCUMENTATION is forwarded, but `use_netrc` is absent — there is no `self.get_option('use_netrc')` call and no `use_netrc=...` kwarg passed to `open_url`. Confirmed by direct read and zero-match grep.

3. **Underlying `open_url` (urls.py:1649-1655)** and **`Request.open` (urls.py:1358-1364)**: Neither signature contains a `use_netrc` parameter. Confirmed by direct read and zero-match grep across the entire file.

The `.netrc` file is therefore consulted unconditionally by `Request.open` (urls.py:1487-1497) whenever no explicit credentials are supplied — the lookup plugin user has no way to suppress or control this behavior.
